title: Слово о пълку Игоревѣ
draftNote: Pilot: the proem only (paragraph 1 of the 1800 edition). This is the first Cyrillic text in Lectorium and the first to use the transliteration line — a proof of the workflow before the rest of the poem.
source: Text: editio princeps — «Ироическая пѣснь о походѣ на половцовъ удѣльнаго князя Новагорода-Сѣверскаго Игоря Святославича», ed. A. I. Musin-Pushkin, Moscow, Senate Press, 1800 (public domain); via Russian Wikisource. Original 1800 orthography preserved (ѣ, ъ, ь, і, titlo-numerals). LINEATION: the 1800 edition prints the poem as continuous prose; we keep that here, segmenting into sense-units at the edition's own punctuation rather than imposing a modern (copyright-era) verse lineation — a deliberate, revisitable choice. TRANSLITERATION: scholarly Slavist romanization (ž š č c x; ě = ѣ; ŭ / ĭ = ъ / ь; ju / ja = ю / я; ī = і-titlo) — a reading aid for the Cyrillic, not a phonetic reconstruction. PRONUNCIATION convention (for eventual audio): reconstructed late-12th-century Old East Slavic, applied consistently. Translations made fresh from the 1800 original; no modern translation was consulted for wording.
about: «Слово о полку Игореве» is a heroic poem about Prince Igor Svyatoslavich's disastrous 1185 campaign against the Polovtsians (Cumans). It survives in NO medieval manuscript: the single copy, found in the 1790s, burned in the Moscow fire of 1812, so every word descends from the 1800 first printed edition and a copy made for Catherine II. Because of that — and because the poem is full of words found nowhere else — many readings are uncertain, a few passages (the famous 'dark places') resist translation altogether, and some scholars have even argued the whole work is an 18th-century forgery (the weight of linguistic evidence says it is genuinely medieval). We read the 1800 text as it stands, mark the doubts honestly in the notes, and never claim more certainty than survives. This is the proem, where the poet weighs how to sing and invokes the old bard Boyan.

sentences.t: Не лѣполи ны бяшетъ, братіе, начяти старыми словесы трудныхъ повѣстій о пълку Игоревѣ, Игоря Святъславлича!
sentences.tr: Ne lěpoli ny bjašetŭ, bratie, načjati starymi slovesy trudnyxŭ pověstij o pŭlku Igorevě, Igorja Svjatŭslavliča!
sentences.l: Were it not fitting for us, brothers, to begin in the old words the grievous tellings of the campaign of Igor, of Igor Svyatoslavich!
sentences.i: Would it not be fitting, brothers, for us to begin in the old manner the hard tale of Igor's campaign — Igor, son of Svyatoslav?
sentences.n: The poem opens by weighing whether to sing in the old bardic style at all. лѣполи = лѣпо ('seemly, fitting') + ли (the question-particle); with бяшетъ (an imperfect of быти) it makes a soft conditional, 'would it (not) be fitting.' пълку here is not 'regiment' but 'campaign / war-band' — the title «о пълку Игоревѣ» is literally 'about Igor's host.' старыми словесы is instrumental plural, 'with old words.'

sentences.t: начати же ся тъй пѣсни по былинамь сего времени, а не по замышленію Бояню.
sentences.tr: načati že sja tŭj pěsni po bylinamĭ sego vremeni, a ne po zamyšleniju Bojanju.
sentences.l: and to begin this song by the true happenings of this time, and not by the design of Boyan.
sentences.i: and to begin this song after the real events of our own time, not after Boyan's flights of fancy.
sentences.n: былина here is NOT the later folk-epic genre; it means 'what actually happened, true events' (from быти 'to be'). The poet promises fact over the old bard's invention (замышленіе). Бояню is a possessive adjective, 'Boyan's,' not a dative noun — you'll meet these -ь/-евъ possessives constantly for names. This plain-truth-vs-ornament contrast programs the whole proem.

sentences.t: Боянъ бо вѣщій, аще кому хотяше пѣснь творити, то растѣкашется мыслію по древу, сѣрымъ вълкомъ по земли, шизымъ орломъ подъ облакы.
sentences.tr: Bojanŭ bo věščij, ašče komu xotjaše pěsnĭ tvoriti, to rastěkašetsja mysliju po drevu, sěrymŭ vŭlkomŭ po zemli, šizymŭ orlomŭ podŭ oblaky.
sentences.l: For Boyan the seer, if he wished to make a song for anyone, then would course in thought over the tree, as a grey wolf over the earth, as a dusky eagle beneath the clouds.
sentences.i: For the prophet Boyan, whenever he wished to make a song for someone, would range in his mind over the tree, like a grey wolf over the land, like a blue-grey eagle beneath the clouds.
sentences.n: The poem's single most debated line — Lectorium's first 'dark place.' The 1800 edition reads мыслію 'in thought'; many scholars emend to мысію ('as a squirrel/marten,' a dialect word), which restores a clean parallel of three animals — squirrel on the tree, wolf on the ground, eagle in the sky. We keep the 1800 reading and flag the doubt: you will meet this squirrel-or-thought question in every serious discussion of the poem. вѣщій ('prophetic, knowing what is hidden') is Boyan's fixed epithet; шизымъ ('blue-grey, dusky') is the poem's colour for eagles.

sentences.t: Помняшеть бо речь първыхъ временъ усобіцѣ;
sentences.tr: Pomnjašetĭ bo rečĭ pŭrvyxŭ vremenŭ usobicě;
sentences.l: For he would recall the speech of the strife of the first times;
sentences.i: For he remembered the tales of the feuds of former days;
sentences.n: усобица = internecine war, the feuding among the Rus' princes — the poem's central grievance, which it blames for Rus' defeats. Помняшеть is an imperfect ('used to remember, kept recalling'); this habitual-past tense runs all through Boyan's description and you'll see the -яше/-ашеть ending again and again.

sentences.t: тогда пущашеть ĩ соколовь на стадо лебедѣй, который дотечаше, та преди пѣсь пояше, старому Ярослову, храброму Мстиславу, иже зарѣза Редедю предъ пълкы Касожьскыми, красному Романови Святъславличю.
sentences.tr: togda puščašetĭ ī sokolovĭ na stado lebeděj, kotoryj dotečaše, ta predi pěsĭ pojaše, staromu Jaroslovu, xrabromu Mstislavu, iže zarěza Rededju predŭ pŭlky Kasožĭskymi, krasnomu Romanovi Svjatŭslavličju.
sentences.l: then he would loose ten falcons upon a flock of swans; whichever one overtook, that one first sang its song — to old Yaroslav, to brave Mstislav, who slew Rededya before the Kasog troops, to fair Roman Svyatoslavich.
sentences.i: then he would loose ten falcons upon a flock of swans, and whichever falcon caught up first, that swan was the first to sing — in praise of old Yaroslav, of brave Mstislav (who slew Rededya before the Kasog army), of handsome Roman Svyatoslavich.
sentences.n: A riddle the poet decodes in the very next line: the 'ten falcons' are Boyan's ten fingers, the 'flock of swans' the harp-strings. ĩ is the letter і under a titlo (an overbar): Old Rus' wrote numbers with letters, and і-titlo = ten. The praise-names are real princes — Mstislav of Tmutarakan famously killed the Kasog (Circassian) champion Rededya in single combat in 1022. Proper names (Ярославъ, Мстиславъ, Редедя, Романъ) are left untranslated and glossed.

sentences.t: Боянъ же, братіе, не ĩ соколовь на стадо лебедѣй пущаше, нъ своя вѣщіа пръсты на живая струны въскладаше;
sentences.tr: Bojanŭ že, bratie, ne ī sokolovĭ na stado lebeděj puščaše, nŭ svoja věščia pŭrsty na živaja struny vŭskladaše;
sentences.l: But Boyan, brothers, did not loose ten falcons upon a flock of swans, but laid his own prophetic fingers upon the living strings;
sentences.i: But Boyan, brothers, did not really loose ten falcons on a flock of swans — he laid his own prophetic fingers on the living strings;
sentences.n: Now the metaphor is undone (нъ 'but'): no real falcons and swans — the fingers ARE the falcons, the strings the swans. живая струны 'the living strings' keeps the swans alive inside the image. пръсты 'fingers' is the old form of перстъ (cf. 'перст'); въскладаше 'laid upon' is again an imperfect.

sentences.t: они же сами Княземъ славу рокотаху.
sentences.tr: oni že sami Knjazemŭ slavu rokotaxu.
sentences.l: and they themselves rumbled glory to the princes.
sentences.i: and the strings themselves thundered out glory to the princes.
sentences.n: они 'they' = the strings, which now seem to sound on their own. рокотати 'to rumble, resound, thrum' is an onomatopoeic word for the harp's deep voice. Княземъ is dative plural, 'to / for the princes.'

glossary.лѣполи: лѣпо + ли — is it (not) fitting? (лѣпо 'seemly, fitting' + question-particle ли)
glossary.бяшетъ: быти — was / would be (imperfect 3sg; here conditional 'would it be')
glossary.пълку: пълкъ — campaign, war-band, host (loc./dat. sg.; root of 'полк')
glossary.святъславлича: Святъславличь — Svyatoslavich, son of Svyatoslav (gen./acc. sg.); patronymic
glossary.начати: начати — to begin (infinitive; here 'let it begin')
glossary.ся: ся — reflexive particle ('-self'); here with начати, 'begin'
glossary.тъй: тъй — that, this (dem., dat. sg. agreeing with пѣсни)
glossary.былинамь: былина — true happenings, real events (instr. pl.; from быти 'to be')
glossary.бояню: Боянь — Boyan's (possessive adj.); of the bard Boyan
glossary.боянъ: Боянъ — Boyan (nom. sg.); the legendary bard
glossary.вѣщій: вѣщій — prophetic; a seer, knowing what is hidden (adj., nom. sg.)
glossary.кому: къто — for whom, for anyone (dat. sg.)
glossary.растѣкашется: растѣкатися — would spread, course, flow out (imperfect 3sg, reflexive)
glossary.мыслію: мысль — in thought (instr. sg.); debated — some emend to мысію 'as a squirrel'
glossary.земли: земля — earth, land (loc./dat. sg., 'over the earth')
glossary.усобіцѣ: усобица — strife, internecine war (gen. sg. / acc. pl.)
glossary.ĩ: і (съ титломъ) — ten (the letter і under a titlo = the numeral 10)
glossary.та: тъ — that one (fem. nom. sg.; = the swan)
glossary.пѣсь: пѣснь — song (acc. sg.; variant of пѣснь)
glossary.ярослову: Ярославъ — to Yaroslav (dat. sg.); proper name
glossary.мстиславу: Мстиславъ — to Mstislav (dat. sg.); prince of Tmutarakan
glossary.редедю: Редедя — Rededya (acc. sg.); the Kasog (Circassian) champion
glossary.романови: Романъ — to Roman (dat. sg.); proper name
glossary.пръсты: пръстъ — fingers (acc. pl.; old form of 'перстъ')
